is_p_falciparum.evidence: Study explicitly assesses P. falciparum drug resistance: 'efficacy and safety of artemether-lumefantrine (AL) for the treatment of uncomplicated falciparum malaria' and 'prevalence of Pfk13 and Pfmdr1 polymorphisms' in P. falciparum.

reports_target_markers.evidence: Reports Pfk13 (codon positions 440–600) and Pfmdr1 (codons N86Y, Y184F, D1246Y). Both genes are in the curated list. Pfk13 position 543 and others in range 440–600 are targets; Pfmdr1 codon 86 is a target marker.

reports_extractable_frequencies.evidence: Provides numerator/denominator counts for marker frequencies: '92.7% (n=319)...successfully sequenced for Pfk13' and '100% (n=344)...for Pfmdr1'; specific mutation counts: '6 (1.9%) samples had non-synonymous mutations' in Pfk13; 'NFD haplotype...detected in 134 (39.0%) samples'; '184F mutant was present between 34.1% (Mlimba) and 46.6% (Mkuzi)' with Table 4 providing per-site counts.

sub_country_location.evidence: Four distinct sentinel sites in different regions: Kibaha (Coastal/Pwani), Mkuzi (Tanga, Muheza district), Mlimba (Morogoro, Kilombero district), Ujiji (Kigoma, Kigoma urban district). Each frequency is tied to a named sub-national site/district, exceeding country-level resolution.

temporal_resolution_within_3y.evidence: Study conducted 'between April and October 2016' and 'between April and September 2016', all samples collected at enrollment (day 0) in 2016. All data span less than one year, well within the 3-year threshold.

african_field_samples.evidence: Study explicitly states samples are from 'four sentinel sites...in mainland Tanzania' with 'human field/clinical isolates': patients 'aged 6 months to 10 years' with confirmed malaria enrolled at health centers (Kibaha, Mkuzi, Mlimba, Ujiji). Samples are from African field settings in Tanzania.

is_primary_study.evidence: Primary empirical study: 'single-arm prospective in vivo study designed to assess the therapeutic efficacy and safety of AL...and the markers of artemisinin and lumefantrine resistance' with 344 patients enrolled and prospective follow-up for 28 days. Not a review, meta-analysis, or modelling paper.

duplicate_risk.evidence: Study was conducted at NMCP sentinel sites as part of routine therapeutic efficacy surveillance (TES). However, no statement of sample overlap with prior large consortia, named cohorts, or multi-site networks beyond these four specific 2016 sites. Standard sentinel-site surveillance with no concrete evidence of duplication with other published datasets.

supplement.evidence: All necessary frequency data are reported in the main text and tables. Table 4 provides per-locus SNP counts by site for Pfmdr1 (N86, 184F, D1246). Pfk13 frequencies are in the text ('6 (1.9%)' non-synonymous mutations, specific variants listed). Sub-national site-level detail is provided for all major findings. No supplementary tables or appendices are referenced as essential for marker frequency extraction.

markers_found: Pfk13: codon positions 440-600 (non-synonymous: R471S, A578S, E433D, I416V, Q613E)
Pfmdr1: N86Y, Y184F, D1246Y (NFD haplotype prevalent at 39.0%)
assessment.summary: Therapeutic efficacy study of artemether-lumefantrine conducted across four sentinel sites in mainland Tanzania (Kibaha, Mkuzi, Mlimba, Ujiji) in 2016. Enrolled 344 patients aged 6 months–10 years with uncomplicated P. falciparum malaria. Reported Pfk13 and Pfmdr1 polymorphism frequencies with full sequencing success (92.7% Pfk13, 100% Pfmdr1). Six non-synonymous Pfk13 mutations detected (1.9%), none previously associated with artemisinin resistance. Pfmdr1 NFD haplotype found in 39.0% of samples (ranging 33.0–45.5% across sites), associated with reduced lumefantrine susceptibility but high treatment efficacy (>98%) observed. All data are sub-nationally resolved and temporally precise (single calendar year).
